Rules Of Engagement
Cell Phones, Twi.er, Facebook
PLEASE TURN OFF
Shut down 	everything 	but your web browser
and MicrosoA Word (or a text editor)

-- 3 of 148 --

Session Structure
40 Minutes of Training
10 Minutes Q&A
10 Minutes Intermission
If I’m on a roll, we may go a bit over, but
we’ll try to keep it on-­‐schedule
We’ll end with an 	op%onal 30-­‐minute
Q&A Session (extended Tme)

-- 4 of 148 --

How To Ask Questions
Please do not raise your hand.
Chat quesTons as we go. QuesTons will be
answered during the 10-­‐Minute Q&A.
We may take some quesTons live and
remove the mute; otherwise Joshua
will answer simple quesTons as we go
and we will cover common quesTons during
the 10-­‐Minute Q&A and the 30-­‐Minute Ending Q&A

-- 5 of 148 --

Our New (But Old) Format
Rather than doing your work online, we will
be using MicrosoA Word (or any text editor) and
you may op%onally use 	Powerpoint or Keynote
Please be sure to save your document every
few seconds (no lost work!)
We’ll have a way for you to walk through this
process 	online 	toward the end of this course.

-- 6 of 148 --

Formatting As You Go?
You Have 2 Op%ons
(1) You can build your enTre 3XVSL Script in Word and
then transfer it over to PowerPoint, Keynote, or a
Google PresentaTon when you’re finished
PROS:
• 	You can see then enTre script all at once and make sure it flows
• 	You can easily move elements around
• 	You are forced to focus more on the content and less on the
layout, which ensures that your copy is powerful and persuasive
CONS:
• 	You have to transfer it all to a Powerpoint, Keynote, or Google
PresentaTon File when you’re finished
• 	Which may mean that you have to copy it all one line at a Tme

-- 7 of 148 --

Formatting As You Go?
You Have 2 Op%ons
(2) You can write the enTre VSL right within your
PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google PresentaTon
Document 	as you go 	(Least Preferred OpTon)
PROS:
• 	All of the script is in your PowerPoint File when you’re
finished so there is minimal copy/paste
CONS:
• 	You will focus less on how to build the content and more
on typing, cut/paste from your previous homework, etc.

-- 8 of 148 --

Assignment Format
All Assignments From This Point
Are Primarily Completed Within Your
PowerPoint or Keynote Document
• 	You should have received and downloaded a copy of this
week’s PowerPoint and/or Keynote file from my email to
you and the Members Area online.
• 	If you did not download this file, you will want to go to
the 5-­‐Step VSL Process Tab in the Members area and
click on the Downloads link in the content area.
• 	Each of the next three weeks, we’ll be building secTons
at a Tme. When complete, it’s simple to merge the three
PowerPoint or Keynote files together.

Review Of The

-- 13 of 148 --

Step 3: The Big Problem
Draws Your Prospect’s APen%on To The Source
Of Their Problem That’s Greater Than Their Pain
This all takes place within the first
few slides of 	The Big Problem:
Crea1ng A Conspiracy

-- 14 of 148 --

The Conspiracy Cycle
The Foundation Of The Big Problem
Your prospect does not just have a problem—
your prospect is involved in a conspiracy
A conspiracy is simply the source of your prospects
withouts (Assignment; Part 3; The Snap SuggesTon)
A reason/cause larger than the prospect’s problem
(Cause over Effect) creates a stronger desire
psychologically to take acTon

-- 15 of 148 --

The Conspiracy Cycle
The Steps To Creation
Step 1:
The Big Lie
Step 2:
It’s Not Your Fault
Step 3:
Blame This
Step 4:
The Big Truth
Step 5:
Open Bigger SoluTon Loop

-- 16 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 1:
Discover Your Lie
Part 2:
TransiTon Into The Lie
Part 3:
Describe The Lie
Part 4:
Label It As False
Part 5:
Results Of Believing This Lie
Part 6:
TransiTon: “Not What I Want For You”

-- 17 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 1: Discover Your Lie
Begin by going to your assignment for
The Snap SuggesTon (Part 3)
In this assignment, you listed benefits
with 2-­‐3 “withouts”
These “withouts” are actually the lies that
your prospect may believe they require to
see results from your product or service

-- 18 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 1: Discover Your Lie
Example:
One of the “withouts” in my Snap SuggesTon was
“…without starving yourself skinny.”
I have now discovered my first lie
I will use it in just a moment
(I’ll show you how and where)

-- 19 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 2: Transition Into The Lie
Example:
The 	Real Problem 	Is The Fact
That You’ve Been 	Lied To 	For Years…
And These Lies Are Keeping You From
The Body You Desire and Deserve…

-- 20 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 3: Describe The Lie
Use the lie you discovered in Part 1
Example:
One Of These Lies Is That You Have To
“Starve Yourself Skinny”…I Bet You’ve
Heard That Or Maybe Even 	Believed
This At One Point In Your Life…
YET:

-- 21 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 3: Describe The Lie
NoTce the emphasis on 	believe and
concluding this slide with an 	anchor drop:
YET:
This creates the forward momentum you
need to inspire conTnuaTon

-- 22 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 4: Label It As False
Here, you simply negate the lie by
saying it’s not true
Example:
This Is Not Even Close To
The Truth…

-- 23 of 148 --

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 5: Results Of Believing The Lie

Technique and create a mini 	Nightmare Story
to describe the consequence of believing the lie

Step 1: The Big Lie
Part 6: Not What I Want For You
Deepen your rapport even further by using a
variaTon of the phrase, “This is not what
I want for you.”
Example:
Listen: This Is Not What I
Want For You, And I Know It’s
Not What You 	Desire 	For Yourself…

-- 29 of 148 --

Step 2: It’s Not Your Fault
Let your viewer know that 	because 	of the
lie, 	it’s not his/her fault for not getting
the desired result
Example:
And, This Is The Reason That Your
Struggle With Weight Loss
Is Not Your Fault…

-- 30 of 148 --

Step 2: It’s Not Your Fault
Another technique is the “I’m Angry” Approach. This
is where you get angry at the lies and the culprits
behind the lies
Example:
“It Really Pisses Me Off That Certain “Forces”
Have 	Conspired 	To Lie To Us…It Makes
Me 	Angry, And It Should Make You
Furious... 	PLUS 	This Is The Reason
Why _____ Is 	Not Your Fault…”

Questions & Answers

-- 32 of 148 --



-- 33 of 148 --

Step 3: Blame 	This
It’s human nature to assign blame. Your
prospects tends to 	blame themselves, 	so you
must take the prospect out of this state in order
to allow him/her to feel empowered when it
comes time to make a decision to take action
3 Parts To Blame This:
If you want to, blame _____
Why 	_____ is to blame
Proof 	_____ is to blame (if possible)

-- 34 of 148 --

Step 3: Blame 	This
Part 1: If You Want To Blame…
Provide something other than their 	self 	to
blame for their pain and lack of results
